fields.summary: Implementing global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py

content:
type: QUESTION
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Are you adding a per-call/local 'filters' parameter to Prompt/from_str/from_file? My global registry needs local filters to take precedence over global ones, want to avoid clobbering your changes to _template_from_str/_template_from_file signatures.

fields.summary: Are you adding a per-call/local 'filters' parameter to Prompt/from_str/from_file? My global registry needs local filters to take precedence over global ones, want to avoid clobbering your changes to _template_from_str/_template_from_file signatures.

content:
type: ANSWER
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: My registry: module-level _FILTER_REGISTRY dict, register_filter(name, func), unregister_filter(name). Currently I do env.filters.update(_FILTER_REGISTRY) before setting built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file. Please have create_jinja_env do: 1) env.filters.update(_FILTER_REGISTRY) (global, lowest precedence), 2) set the built-in filters, 3) env.filters.update(filters or {}) (per-call, highest precedence) - so per-call > builtins > global. I'll adapt _FILTER_REGISTRY usage to fit inside your create_jinja_env once you land it, just ping me when it's ready so we don't clobber each other's edits.

content:

files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Done: added _FILTER_REGISTRY dict + register_filter()/unregister_filter() in outlines/prompts.py; merged into env.filters in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file (registry applied before built-ins, so built-ins can't be shadowed). Self-contained, doesn't depend on a shared create_jinja_env helper. Patch written to patch.txt.
